Add necessary columns to the table.
The question asks about which song has the most featured guests. We need to know the number of featured guests for each song. We extract the value from column "featured guest(s)" and create a different column "number of featured guests" for each row. The datatype is numerical.

Filter out useless columns.
similar words of the question link to columns:
song -> title
featured guests -> featured guest(s)
column value of the question link to columns:
None
semantic sentence of the question link to columns:
which song has the most featured guests -> title, featured guest(s)

The question is asking to determine which song has the most featured guests. To find out, we need to count the number of featured guests for each song (by checking how many names are listed in the "featured guest(s)" column) and then sort the data based on this count in the order of "large to small" using the f_sort_column(count(featured guest(s))) operation. This will allow us to see which song has the most featured guests at the top of the sorted list.

The question is asking to determine which song has the highest number of featured guests. Since each row represents a song and the "featured guest(s)" column lists the guest artists for each song, we need to examine the number of guests listed per song. We can analyze the "featured guest(s)" column to count the number of guests for each song and identify the one with the highest count. We can use the f_count_guests(featured guest(s)) operation to count the guests for each song.

"The Shadiest One" has 1 featured guest (CJ Mac), "Can't Hold Back" has 1 featured guest (Ice Cube), "Keep Hustlin" has 2 featured guests (E-40 & Too Short), "Like That" has 3 featured guests (Ice Cube, Daz Dillinger & CJ Mac), "Cheddar" has 2 featured guests (Mack 10 & Ice Cube), and "Better Days" has 1 featured guest (Ron Banks). Therefore, "Like That" has the most featured guests with 3.
